codeReview.summary: Worker expanded SPEC.md prose sections from 6 to 13 state types (v1.1), but the embedded JSON schema at SPEC.md lines 615-617 was NOT updated. It still contains only 6 types: ["loading", "empty", "error", "success", "offline", "permission-denied"]. The schema.json file has 13 types. VAL-SPEC-001 requires 'No types present in one but not the other' — the embedded schema in SPEC.md must match schema.json exactly.

issues.description: Embedded JSON schema 'type' enum has only 6 values: ["loading", "empty", "error", "success", "offline", "permission-denied"]. schema.json has 13 values including the 7 extended types (idle, scrollback, editing, search-active, streaming, refreshing, field-selected). VAL-SPEC-001 fails because the embedded schema in SPEC.md does not match schema.json.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker updated prose sections (Section 9.2.1, Section 9.3 table, Appendix A) to 13 types but missed the embedded JSON schema (YAML code block at end of SPEC.md). The diff confirms only 37 lines were inserted affecting prose sections, not the embedded schema JSON. No validation caught this discrepancy.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git diff 90ad989 shows prose changes but embedded schema enum unchanged. SPEC.md line 617 shows enum with 6 values.

summary: FAIL - The fix partially addresses the original failure (prose sections now show 13 types), but introduces a new blocking issue: the embedded JSON schema in SPEC.md (lines 615-617) still has only 6 types while schema.json has 13. VAL-SPEC-001 explicitly requires no discrepancy between SPEC.md state type enum and schema.json state type enum. The embedded schema must be updated to include all 13 types.
